user_query:
I’m preparing a sequence on samizdat and show trials for my documentary and need two things grounded in the local research files.

Part A — Extract grounded quotes
- Recursively scan all text/markdown files under input/research/ (including subfolders) and read their contents.
- Identify verbatim quotes that mention either "samizdat" or "trial" (case-insensitive). Treat a line as a quote if it either:
  1) contains text enclosed in straight double quotes "…", or
  2) is a markdown blockquote line that begins with "> ".
- For each matching quote, capture the following fields:
  - source_file: the relative path to the file (e.g., input/research/transcripts/sinyavsky_1966_trial.txt)
  - line_start: the 1-based line number where the quote begins
  - line_end: the 1-based line number where the quote ends (same as start for single-line quotes)
  - speaker_or_attribution: if the same line contains an em dash (—), use the text after the dash (trim trailing spaces and any trailing year in parentheses); otherwise "unknown"
  - year: if the same line contains a 4-digit year (e.g., 1966, 1972), record it; otherwise "unknown"
  - quote_text: the quoted text without the surrounding literal quote characters; for markdown blockquotes, omit the leading "> "
- Deduplicate by quote_text (case-sensitive exact match): if the same quote_text appears more than once across files, keep only the first occurrence.
- Write the results to output/extracted_quotes.csv with columns in this order and exact header: source_file,line_start,line_end,speaker_or_attribution,year,quote_text
- Also write output/extraction_report.json with:
  - total_files_scanned (integer)
  - total_quotes_extracted (after deduplication)
  - per_file_counts: an object mapping each scanned file path to the number of matching quotes found before deduplication.

Part B — Rewrite the outreach email
- Open input/outreach_draft.txt. Keep the first line beginning with "Subject:" exactly unchanged. Rewrite only the body below it.
- Produce a single-paragraph body of at most 120 words in a professional, neutral tone.
- The rewritten body must explicitly include all of the following: (1) the project title 'Dissident Voices', (2) the materials 'trial transcript excerpts' and 'samizdat passages (Chronicle of Current Events)', (3) that the use is non-commercial educational, (4) a request for reply by 'May 30', and (5) the contact email 'filmmaker@example.org'.
- Save the combined output (original Subject line followed by the rewritten body) to output/outreach_email_rewrite.txt.

Please ensure both outputs are created and that all paths match exactly. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
